channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: A difference-in-differences analysis of 62,217 Medicare beneficiaries found that the implementation of the Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) bundled payment model did not significantly increase the proportion of hospitals delivering prehabilitation for lower extremity joint replacement.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effectiveness of an individualised swimming and education programme for chronic low back pain (EduSwim): a randomised controlled trial.
item.description: This RCT assessed an individualized swimming and education program for chronic low back pain compared to education alone, delivered via telehealth sessions over 8 weeks, with disability (RMDQ) as the primary outcome.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: APTA Members Unite to Advance Payment Advocacy Via New SPARC Forum and Pledge
item.description: APTA members are uniting to advance payment advocacy through the newly formed SPARC Forum and a pledge to support these efforts.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Virtual Reality-assisted Physiotherapeutic Training for Patients With Knee Osteoarthritis: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 5 RCTs found that virtual reality (VR)-assisted physiotherapy significantly improved pain (SMD = 0.40), physical function (SMD = 0.37), and balance (SMD = 0.44) in patients with knee osteoarthritis compared to traditional rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Association of combined physical and cognitive rehabilitation with post-stroke dementia: a U.S. multicenter retrospective cohort study.
item.description: A U.S. multicenter retrospective cohort study (n=958) found that combined physical and cognitive rehabilitation within six months after stroke significantly reduced the risk of post-stroke dementia compared to physical rehabilitation alone (HR = 0.65, 95% CI: 0.54-0.79, P < 0.001).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Oxford visual perception screen detects visual perception difficulties in sub-acute adult stroke survivors: an early cross-sectional diagnostic accuracy study.
item.description: A prospective cross-sectional diagnostic accuracy study (n=unknown) in sub-acute adult stroke survivors in the UK found that the Oxford Visual Perception Screen (OxVPS) effectively detected visual perception difficulties, aligning with clinical guidelines.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Measurement Characteristics and Clinical Utility of the 6-Minute Walk Test Among People with Stroke
item.description: A new article evaluates the measurement characteristics and clinical utility of the 6-Minute Walk Test (6MWT) for individuals with stroke, providing insights for its application in rehabilitation.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: The RESOLVE Trial: A Stepped-Wedge, Cluster-Randomized Clinical Trial Evaluating the Effects of Clinical Practice Guidelines and Psychologically Informed Physical Therapy on Low Back Pain Outcomes in Service Members and Veterans.
item.description: A multisite, stepped-wedge, cluster-randomized trial found that implementing a combined clinical practice guideline and psychologically informed physical therapy (CPG+PIPT) strategy significantly reduced low back pain-related disability, pain, and downstream healthcare utilization in service members and veterans.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: From mechanics to mobilization: a biomechanically-informed rehabilitation framework for cesarean-related scars | BMC Women's Health
item.description: A systematic review proposed a biomechanically-informed rehabilitation framework for cesarean-related scars, integrating strategies for both abdominal wall and uterine scars, addressing a lack of standardization in current approaches.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: A systematic review of current evidence on clinical outcomes of lumbar roll and modified SIMS manipulation techniques in sacroiliac joint dysfunction.
item.description: This systematic review evaluated the clinical efficacy of lumbar roll and modified SIMS manipulation techniques for sacroiliac joint dysfunction, searching databases for studies published between January 2021 and December 2025.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: In adult patients with brainstem strokes, does dysphagia rehabilitation improve health-related outcomes compared to usual care for inpatients? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis with 12 studies and 443 participants explored the effectiveness of dysphagia rehabilitation in inpatients with brainstem stroke, categorizing interventions into traditional swallowing therapy or device inclusion.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Long-term benefits of ultrasound-guided percutaneous electrical nerve stimulation for chronic lateral elbow pain: a randomized controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized, double-blinded, sham-controlled trial with 49 participants found that ultrasound-guided percutaneous electrical nerve stimulation applied to the radial nerve, combined with exercise, provided mid- and long-term benefits for chronic lateral elbow pain, improving pain intensity, disability, and psychological outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: A Multimodal Assessment of Gastric Motility, reflux risk, and Clinical Efficacy: Intermittent Oro-Esophageal versus Nasogastric Tube Feeding for Post-Stroke Dysphagia in a Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A single-center randomized controlled trial (n=54) in China found that intermittent oro-esophageal tube feeding was more efficacious than nasogastric tube feeding in reducing reflux risk and supporting swallowing recovery in patients with post-stroke dysphagia over 14 days.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The association between diabetes mellitus and low back pain: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis found a significant association between diabetes mellitus and low back pain, with a higher comorbid prevalence and longitudinal relationship between the two conditions in adult populations.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Applications of ICF-Based Assessments and Interventions in Post-Stroke Rehabilitation: A Narrative Review.
item.description: A narrative review identified 546 ICF-based assessments across 166 studies in post-stroke rehabilitation, demonstrating over 80% overlap with topics in the United Kingdom Stroke Guideline, primarily focusing on Body Function.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Kinesiotaping May Reduce Early Swelling After Knee Replacement, states study
item.description: A randomized study of 136 patients found that adding kinesiotaping to standard rehabilitation after total knee arthroplasty reduced early postoperative knee swelling and led to a modest improvement in knee flexion.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Effects of square-step exercise on fear of falling in older patients with ischemic stroke: a cluster randomized controlled trial | BMC Geriatrics
item.description: A cluster randomized controlled trial involving 62 older ischemic stroke patients found that square-step exercise improved fall fear, balance, and activities of daily living, and reduced fall risk.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: γ-transcranial alternating current stimulation improves upper-limb motor function in stroke patients and modulates oscillatory imbalance: a pilot randomized controlled trial | Journal of NeuroEngineering and Rehabilitation
item.description: A pilot randomized controlled trial demonstrated that γ-transcranial alternating current stimulation improved upper-limb motor function in stroke patients and modulated oscillatory imbalance.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: The effect of an eight-week FIFA 11+ program on the landing error scoring system following functional fatigue in adolescent female soccer players with dynamic knee valgus | BMC Sports Science, Medicine and Rehabilitation
item.description: An eight-week FIFA 11+ program significantly improved the Landing Error Scoring System (LESS) following functional fatigue in adolescent female soccer players with dynamic knee valgus.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Let's talk about sex (and gender) after ACL injury: a ...
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis from 2023 investigated sex and gender differences in self-reported activity and knee-related outcomes following anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) injury.
item.author: BJSM
